name: Fruit Cake
recipeIngredient: 1/2 cup sugar (white sugar can be used)
2 1/2 cups mixed dried fruit (half currants and half raisins)
1 1/2 cups white flour (whole wheat is acceptable)
recipeInstructions:
1. Combine the apple sauce, water, sugar, and dried fruit in a saucepan. Bring to a boil and simmer for 20 minutes, then allow to cool.
2. Preheat the oven to 300°F. Line a 6-inch round cake pan with greaseproof paper.
3. Mix the egg whites into the fruit mixture, then add the flour and baking powder. Blend until a stiff batter forms; adjust with water if necessary.
4. Pour the batter into the prepared pan and sprinkle brown sugar on top.
5. Bake for 1 1/2 to 2 hours, or until a skewer inserted into the center comes out clean. Note that due to the fruit content, it may not come out perfectly clean.
6. The type and amount of fruit can be varied to create different flavors and textures. Options include substituting chopped glazed cherries for some fruit, adding candied orange peel or dried fruits like dates and apricots.
